expectations.evidence: The reply lists more than four distinct questions across categories like dashboard scope, frequency, timing, format, delivery, failure handling, and lifecycle.
expectations.text: The answer is a set of at least four distinct questions.

expectations.evidence: Questions on timezone ('Whose timezone defines "overnight"...'), PDF fidelity ('Does the PDF need to visually mirror the dashboard...'), frequency options ('What exact frequency options do we support...'), and failure/retry ('If generation or sending fails on a given night, do we retry...') are all directly grounded in the spec.
expectations.text: At least three questions are grounded in specifics of this spec (e.g. overnight timing or time zones, CSV vs PDF, frequency options, failure/retry, large exports, who can access the data, retention).

expectations.evidence: The lifecycle bullet combines two unrelated asks in one sentence: 'Can a user have more than one schedule at a time, and can they edit or pause one after creating it, or is it create/delete only?' bundles the multi-schedule question with the edit/pause question.
expectations.text: No single question crams two unrelated asks together — each question carries one idea.

expectations.evidence: Questions like 'Does the PDF need to visually mirror the dashboard (charts, layout, branding)...' and 'Whose timezone defines "overnight" and "next morning"' are tailored to this exact dashboard-export-to-email feature, not boilerplate.
expectations.text: The questions are specific to this feature rather than a generic export checklist.
